title: acer glabrum subsp. douglasii
intro: Acer glabrum subsp. douglasii, also known as Douglas maple, is a plant species within the family Sapindaceae, native to the western regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, with medicinal properties including its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Douglas maple include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Douglas maple can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile ingredient in natural remedies.
